# daytona_organization_invitation (Resource)

Manages a Daytona organization invitation.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "daytona_organization_invitation" "example" {
  organization_id   = "organization-id"
  email             = "user@example.com"
  role              = "member"
  assigned_role_ids = []
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `assigned_role_ids` (Set of String) Custom organization role IDs assigned to the invitee. Use an empty set for no custom roles.
- `email` (String) Email address to invite.
- `organization_id` (String) Daytona organization ID.
- `role` (String) Organization member role for the invitee.

### Optional

- `expires_at` (String) RFC3339 expiration timestamp for the invitation. Defaults to a Daytona-assigned expiry when not set.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) Invitation creation timestamp.
- `id` (String) Daytona organization invitation ID.
- `invited_by` (String) Email address of the inviter.
- `organization_name` (String) Daytona organization name.
- `status` (String) Invitation status.
- `updated_at` (String) Invitation update timestamp.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import daytona_organization_invitation.example organization-id/invitation-id
```
